request.question: What is the type of musical composition that was used by the Korean female band Isak N Jiyeon?

title: Isak N Jiyeon
List of musical forms by era
Pulitzer Prize for Music
Kenzie (songwriter)
Park Kyung-won
Duet
Schillinger System
Lina (South Korean singer)
4 Intimate Nights with Beyoncé
Tell Me Baby (album)
sentences: Isak N Jiyeon (이삭 N 지연) was a Korean female duo formed by SM Entertainment in 2002 that has since been disbanded.
Jiyeon was involved in SM's project named The Grace, a four-member Korean girl group that sings, dances and acts.

Isak was featured in the SM Town albums and videos and continued to do a variety of work, such as being DJ and VJ for Arirang TV, MC work for various networks, and taking on small acting roles on network television.
This is a list of musical forms and genres organized according to the eras of Classical music.
The form of a musical composition refers to the general outline of the composition, based on the sections that comprise it or on specific details that are unique to a certain type of composition.

For example, a rondo is based on alternation between familiar and novel sections (ABACA); a mazurka is defined by its meter and rhythm; a nocturne is based on the mood it creates, required to be inspired by or evocative of night.
This list summarizes these broadly defined forms and genres within the musical periods that they arose or became common.
The Pulitzer Prize for Music is one of the seven American Pulitzer Prizes that are annually awarded for Letters, Drama, and Music.
It was first awarded in 1943.
Joseph Pulitzer did not call for such a prize in his will, but had arranged for a music scholarship to be awarded each year.

This was eventually converted into a full-fledged prize: "For a distinguished musical composition of significant dimension by an American that has had its first performance in the United States during the year."
Because of the requirement that the composition had its world premiere during the year of its award, the winning work had rarely been recorded and sometimes had received only one performance.
In 2004 the terms were modified to read: "For a distinguished musical composition by an American that has had its first performance or recording in the United States during the year."
Kenzie (Korean 켄지 "Kenji", born Kim Yeon Jung, February 3, 1976 in Shimane-ken, Japan) is a composer signed under S.M. Entertainment.
She has composed dozens of songs for S.M. label-mates including BoA, Isak N Jiyeon, Super Junior, The Grace, TVXQ, Girls' Generation, SHINee, f(x), EXO, Red Velvet and NCT.

Park Kyung-won (24 June 1901 – 7 August 1933) was, along with Kwon Ki-ok of the Republic of China Air Force, one of the earliest Korean female aviators. Though it is generally agreed that Kwon was the first female pilot, Park is still recognised as the first Korean female civilian pilot, as Kwon was trained by the Republic of China Air Force.
She was the subject of the controversial 2005 South Korean film "Blue Swallow", in which she was portrayed by actress Jang Jin-young.
A duet is a musical composition for two performers in which the performers have equal importance to the piece, often a composition involving two singers or two pianists.
It differs from a harmony, as the performers take turns performing a solo section rather than performing simultaneously.
A piece performed by two pianists performing together on the same piano is a "piano duet" or "piano four hands".
A piece for two pianists performing together on separate pianos is a "piano duo".
The Schillinger System of Musical Composition, named after Joseph Schillinger (1895–1943) is a method of musical composition based on mathematical processes.
It comprises theories of rhythm, harmony, melody, counterpoint, form, and semantics (emotional meaning, as in movie music).
Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.
She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.
She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.
She currently active as a musical actress.
4 Intimate Nights with Beyoncé was the second residency show by American recording artist Beyoncé.
Held during four non-consecutive nights in August 2011 at the Roseland Ballroom in New York City, the concerts were part of Beyoncé's campaign in support of her fourth studio album "4" (2011).
All the songs on the standard version of the album, excluding "Start Over", were performed by her to a standing room-only audience of 3,200.
Beyoncé also sang some of her previous hits from her three prior studio albums as well as songs she recorded with former girl group Destiny's Child in the 1990s and early 2000s.
Wearing a linky gold sparkling mini-dress, she was backed by four female dancers and a 20-piece female band including a horn and orchestra section.
Tell Me Baby is the debut and only Korean studio album by South Korean duo Isak N Jiyeon, a new 2002 group from SM Entertainment, released on September 3, 2002.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question` and `passages`, produce the single field `summary`.

Goal: compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a short intermediate state that makes it easy for the next-hop agent to (a) see what facts are present per candidate/entity, (b) see what is ambiguous or missing, and (c) know 1–4 prioritized next-hop retrieval targets or concise queries. This module does not produce the final answer unless the provided passages already unambiguously contain every fact required to resolve the question's relation for every argument.

Procedure:
1) Decide whether the question's relation is semantically COMPLETE given the supplied passages. Treat the hop as INCOMPLETE if any of the following hold: a) the relation requires combining or comparing facts across pages (e.g., comparing birth dates, publication/founding years, ordinal ranks, confirming a named director among candidates) and the passages do not supply each required fact unambiguously for all relevant arguments; b) any relation argument is ambiguous or has multiple candidate pages or mentions in the passages; c) an expected candidate page or explicit fact for an argument is missing (e.g., only one side of a comparison is present). Do not trigger INCOMPLETE solely from surface tokens — base the decision on whether the relation's arguments are unambiguously resolved.

   - Narrowed trigger for absent question-named candidates: mark a named candidate as "absent and needing a page/bridge" only when (i) that question-named candidate is a semantically required argument to resolve the relation (the relation cannot be resolved without confirming a fact about that candidate), AND (ii) the supplied passages do not already contain any unambiguous short factual token (for example, an explicit date, direct property token, or relation-token) that would fully answer this hop. If the passages already include such an explicit token that resolves the relation, do NOT mark the hop INCOMPLETE merely because a candidate page is missing.

2) If the hop is COMPLETE (all required facts to resolve the relation for every argument are present unambiguously in the passages): produce a concise derived result in `summary`. This may be a short phrase or sentence that states the necessary fact(s) needed by the pipeline. Keep it minimal and grounded in the passages; you may include a 1–2 token citation (passage title) if helpful.

3) If the hop is INCOMPLETE or AMBIGUOUS: do NOT assert a final answer or perform the final comparison/selection. Instead emit a short structured report in `summary` containing these labeled elements (prefer bullets or a compact paragraph with explicit labels). Keep all text neutral and non-assertive.

  - evidence: For each named candidate/argument mentioned in the question or passages, list the candidate label and the exact passage title(s) that mention it plus a very short quoted snippet or explicit fact token found there (1–12 words). If a candidate is not mentioned, say so briefly.

  - ambiguities_or_missing: Explicitly name any ambiguities (e.g., multiple candidates for the same name, multiple pages that could be the intended entity) and any missing expected pages or facts.

  - missing_facts_or_bridge: State exactly which fact(s) or disambiguation is needed to complete the relation (e.g., "birth date for Person A", "publication year of Work B", "director credit for Film C"). Be as specific and minimal as possible.

  - retrieval_direction: Provide 1–4 prioritized next-hop targets or concise queries to resolve the missing facts/ambiguities. Apply this dynamic prioritization policy:
      a) If the missing fact is biographical or identity information about a semantically required question-named candidate (examples: birth date, occupation, nationality, canonical identity that the relation depends on), prioritize retrieving the candidate's canonical biography or main entity page first (exact title when known).
      b) If the missing fact is a property of another entity or a document type that typically holds the property (examples: game developer, layout engine used by a site, race event date, event report), prioritize a concise property-focused query or the canonical page that directly carries that property (examples: "[Game] developer", "[Browser] engine", "2009 Belgian Grand Prix").
      c) If name-disambiguation is suspected (same or similar names present on-page or multiple candidate pages could match), explicitly note the ambiguity and prioritize an exact candidate-title retrieval and a short disambiguation query (e.g., "<Name> (occupation)" or "<Name> disambiguation").
      d) Never prefer a biography-first retrieval when the missing item is better recovered by a targeted property query or when passages already include an explicit relation token that completes the hop.

Formatting and style constraints (apply to both COMPLETE and INCOMPLETE cases):
- The output must be a single `summary` field only. Do not emit other fields.
- In INCOMPLETE reports, use neutral, non-assertive phrasing. Do not produce final comparisons, numeric answers, or definitive selections (e.g., avoid "X is the oldest" or "Answer: 3").
- Keep the report compact and actionable (short labeled bullets or one concise paragraph). Avoid long prose.
- It is allowed and encouraged to quote short factual snippets from passages and cite passage titles to show grounding. Grounding snippets must be ≤12 words.
- Do not defer or mark INCOMPLETE when the passages already unambiguously provide all facts for the relation and its arguments.

Produce the `summary` content that follows these rules.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the single field `query`.

Goal
- Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-intent query suitable for a second-hop search that will recover the missing bridging evidence needed to answer the question. Do not produce the final answer in `query`.

Semantic decision rule (when to emit a retrieval-intent query)
- If summary_1 does NOT assert a confirmed linking entity or verified bridging fact needed to answer the question, you MUST emit exactly one compact retrieval-intent query. "Does not assert a confirmed bridge" includes short or candidate-only summaries (e.g., a single named entity, a quoted candidate title, or numeric anchors that are not verified) and any case where the visible anchors do not unambiguously identify the needed bridge.
- If summary_1 already names a confirmed bridge (a clearly identified, verified linking entity or unambiguous metadata that directly connects the question's pieces), you may produce a compact query to fetch supporting evidence, but do NOT produce an answer in the `query` field.
- If summary_1 explicitly states that the bridge cannot be recovered automatically (extreme ambiguity or user-local context requiring clarification), do NOT force a misleading retrieval query. In that case, set `query` to the single short token BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE (all caps, no punctuation) so downstream steps can prompt the user for clarification. Do not invent facts.

Query construction rules
- Output exactly one compact line of text as `query`. Keep it short and retrieval-focused (few keywords, no full sentences). Use visible anchors from `question` and `summary_1` and minimal retrieval keywords that express the missing relation (e.g., anchor1 anchor2 which artist recorded both; AlbumTitle album artist genre; 1991–92 NHL season regular season).
- Construct the query as a single noun-phrase: a canonical anchor OR an anchor paired with a minimal disambiguator (anchor + short parenthetical/occupation/identifier) optionally followed by a single concise relation-noun when the missing bridge is a specific temporal or numeric attribute.
  - Only add a minimal disambiguator when the visible anchor is ambiguous and the disambiguator itself is explicitly present or strongly implied in `question` or `summary_1`. Do not add disambiguators when the anchor is already unambiguous or the bridge is already confirmed.
  - Relation additions are allowed only as a single neutral relation-noun that names the target attribute (examples: "birth year", "publication date", "species count"). Do NOT use verb-like or assertive relation phrasing (forbid words/phrases such as "born", "birth name", "aka", "founder", "founded", "is the").
  - The final query should look like: "Anchor" OR "Anchor Disambiguator" OR "Anchor Disambiguator relation-noun" (all as a short noun-phrase).
- Do not include the final answer, assertions, or user-facing instructions in the `query` field. Avoid polite phrasing, questions to the user, or explanatory text. Prefer noun phrases and relation keywords.
- If your natural impulse would be to write a declarative answer or a clarification sentence in `query`, instead convert that output into a compact retrieval-intent query that targets the missing bridge.
- Preserve semantic fidelity: base the query only on visible anchors and relations in `question` and `summary_1`. Do not invent hidden identifiers or assume unseen facts.

Format contract
- Return exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short string. If unrecoverable as described above, set `query` to BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE.

Examples of disallowed outputs for `query`
- A declarative answer (e.g., "The artist was X.")
- A user-facing clarification sentence (e.g., "Do you mean X or Y?") unless summary_1 explicitly requires clarification — in that case use BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE.

Behavioral constraints
- Do not use surface heuristics (token count, punctuation, or quotation marks) as the trigger; apply the semantic rule above.
- Emit only one compact retrieval string when the bridge is missing; do not emit multiple queries or long explanations.

Inputs available: `question`, `summary_1`. Output required: single field `query`.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`. The `summary` must be a concise, post-retrieval integration of the evidence that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Produce only the `summary` field as the integrated output string. Follow these rules when composing `summary`:

- Purpose: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` into a grounded state that retains or resolves the earlier bridge only when the second-hop passages supply relation- or attribute-facts that are necessary to answer or disambiguate the `question` (for example: partnership/membership links, joint-award partners indicated by 'with', or requested biographical qualifiers such as nationality, birthdate, current team/status).

- Primary output: The summary's primary output should be the concise canonical label or token drawn exactly from `context` (do not change which label/name/token was selected by earlier answer-selection logic).

- When to append a supporting sentence: Append a single short, passage-grounded supporting sentence only when all of these three conditions hold:
  1) The `question`'s semantic intent genuinely requires a relation- or attribute-fact to justify or disambiguate the canonical label from `context` (not when the question is a pure identification/token-extraction or simple binary label with no disambiguating need).
  2) At least one retrieved `passage` contains an explicit, unambiguous predication that links the exact canonical label (or a clearly corroborated alias) to the required relation or attribute. The passage must state the link directly (examples of acceptable forms: 'X is a member of Y', 'X formed in YEAR', 'X was born DATE', 'X with Y', 'X is the Y of Z'). Mere co-occurrence, implication, or mention of the attribute elsewhere on the page is insufficient.
  3) Identity alignment is verified: confirm that the passage's assertion refers to the same entity token in `context` by matching the exact name or an alias that is explicitly corroborated in the passages (via roles, dates, titles, or other unambiguous attributes). If identity is ambiguous or the passage links the attribute to a different named entity, do not append.

- Content and form of the appended sentence: If appending, produce one short sentence that (a) explicitly names the canonical label or the corroborated alias and (b) states the direct relation/attribute exactly as asserted by the supporting passage (paraphrase briefly; do not invent or speculate). Keep the added sentence tightly focused and grounded in the passage wording; avoid long quotations. The entire `summary` (primary label plus appended sentence) must be concise and limited to 1–3 sentences total.

- When not to append: Do not append a supporting sentence and instead return only the canonical label/token when any of the following apply:
  - The question expects a normalized single-token identifier or binary/multiple-choice label and no passage-stated relation is required to disambiguate it.
  - The `context` already contains a full, passage-consistent, multi-clause integrated answer for that question.
  - The supporting passage does not explicitly predicate the required relation/attribute of the same named entity/clearly corroborated alias in `context` (including cases of mere co-occurrence or indirect/mediated implication).
  - Identity alignment is ambiguous or ties the attribute to another entity.

- Transitive/alias caution: Do not collapse mediated or transitive links into a direct equivalence unless each necessary link is explicitly and unambiguously asserted in the provided passages and clearly refers to the same entity. If the relation would require chaining two separate assertions, both must be present and identity-aligned; otherwise refrain from asserting the transitive equivalence.

- Insufficient information: If `context` and `passages` together do not supply enough explicit, identity-aligned information to resolve the required relation/attribute, summarize the remaining uncertainty succinctly rather than asserting unsupported facts.

- Style and constraints: Keep output concise. Do not invent facts or add unsupported inferences. Avoid long verbatim quotations. Do not change which label or name is selected by the existing answer-selection logic. Produce only the `summary` field (a single string) as the integrated output.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use only summary_1 and summary_2 as evidence. Treat summary_2 as the later, integrated evidence and prioritize it when reconciling information; use summary_1 only as the earlier, pre-second-hop context. Do not assume the final state is unresolved solely because summary_1 contains bridging or retrieval-oriented language.

When summary_2 lists multiple co-referent lexical variants for the same entity, prefer the shortest explicit canonical variant only if all of the following hold: (1) that shorter variant appears verbatim in summary_2; (2) removing the longer form's additional qualifiers (parentheticals, locative suffixes, administrative/categorical words, punctuation/abbreviation markers) would not delete tokens the question explicitly requires or would not create ambiguity among entities visible in the summaries; and (3) the shorter variant still uniquely identifies the intended referent within the visible summaries. If any of these conditions fails, emit the form from summary_2 exactly, preserving parentheticals, punctuation, administrative/categorical tokens, multi-component place formatting, and parenthetical acronyms. Base these choices on semantic content and uniqueness in the visible evidence; do not apply surface heuristics that blindly strip parentheses, punctuation, locative suffixes, legal or category words, or other disambiguating tokens.

Produce exactly one field, answer, whose value is the minimal final answer string that is fully supported by the visible summaries. The answer field must contain only that minimal plain-text string and nothing else (no explanation, no surrounding quotes, no JSON wrappers, no debugging or transport artifacts).

Decide whether the question's intent is binary (a truth-value / yes-no question) or non-binary (asking for a name, title, date, place, numeric count, selection, or other factual value). Make this decision semantically (based on the question's meaning), not by surface heuristics like token counts or punctuation.

- If the question's intent is binary AND both summary_1 and summary_2 unambiguously agree on the same truth-value, emit exactly one lowercase token: either yes or no (and nothing else) as the answer value.

- For all other question intents (non-binary), emit the minimal factual string supported by the summaries exactly as needed: preserve multi-token values, necessary punctuation, full date formats, and place formats (for example, full names, "City, State", or "Month Day, Year"), and do not truncate these to a single token.

If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not jointly support an unambiguous minimal answer, do not fabricate or over-assert; follow the system's established fallback behavior for ambiguous or unsupported questions.

Always forbid emitting serialized queries, JSON/dict-like structures, internal-debug wrappers, or other transport artifacts as the final answer. The answer must be plain text only.

Make all judgments about minimality and agreement based on the semantic content of summary_1 and summary_2, with priority to summary_2 when they differ.

Produce the answer value exactly as specified above in the answer field.

In 2004 the terms were modified to read: "For a distinguished musical composition by an American that has had its first performance or recording in the United States during the year."

Jiyeon was involved in SM's project named The Grace, a four-member Korean girl group that sings, dances and acts.  Isak was featured in the SM Town albums and videos and continued to do a variety of work, such as being DJ and VJ for Arirang TV, MC work for various networks, and taking on small acting roles on network television.

Lina (South Korean singer) | Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.  She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.  She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.  She currently active as a musical actress.
Tell Me Baby (album) | Tell Me Baby is the debut and only Korean studio album by South Korean duo Isak N Jiyeon, a new 2002 group from SM Entertainment, released on September 3, 2002.

Kenzie (songwriter) | Kenzie (Korean 켄지 "Kenji", born Kim Yeon Jung, February 3, 1976 in Shimane-ken, Japan) is a composer signed under S.M. Entertainment.  She has composed dozens of songs for S.M. label-mates including BoA, Isak N Jiyeon, Super Junior, The Grace, TVXQ, Girls' Generation, SHINee, f(x), EXO, Red Velvet and NCT.

Kim Isak | Kim Isak was born Ida Dene Simmons on May 25, 1985 in Frankfurt, Germany.  She left Germany and moved to Kansas with her mother.  She left Kansas when she was 4 1/2 years old to move to California and lived there until 1999.  She emigrated to Korea later to learn about heritage and explore her music career in Korean pop music.  Isak lived in a mostly Caucasian community with her mother.

TTL (Time to Love) | "TTL (Time to Love)" is a hit single by South Korean girl group T-ara and boy band Supernova.  It includes members Eunjung, Hyomin, Soyeon, and Jiyeon of T-ara and Kwangsu, Jihyuk, and Geonil of Supernova.

Colonial Song | Colonial Song is a musical composition written by Australian composer Percy Grainger.  Although Grainger created versions for different types of musical ensembles, its most commonly used version today is for concert band.

Isak was featured in the SM Town albums and videos and continued to do a variety of work, such as being DJ and VJ for Arirang TV, MC work for various networks, and taking on small acting roles on network television.

She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.  She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.  She currently active as a musical actress.
Isaqlı | Isaqlı (also, İsaqlı, Isak-Kishlak, Isak-Kishtak, and Isakly) is a village in the Jabrayil Rayon of Azerbaijan.

UWC ISAK Japan | UWC ISAK Japan (ISAK) (Japanese: ユナイテッド・ワールド・カレッジISAKジャパン) is a pre-university international boarding school located in Karuizawa, Japan.  UWC ISAK Japan is the 17th member of the United World Colleges movement.  The mission of the UWC movement and of the school is to "make education a force to unite people, nations and cultures for peace and a sustainable future".  UWC ISAK Japan is accredited by Japan’s Ministry of Education (MEXT), and teaches the International Baccalaureate Diploma Program years one and two.  Additionally, the school offers a pre-IB diploma year.
Isak Konon | Isak Konon Wombon or Isak Konon (born 15 February 1986 in Merauke, Papua) is an Indonesian football defender who can operate as a winger as well.  He currently plays for Persiwa Wamena.
